messages.content:
Savey said on 01 December 2023

It appears the BBC One Christmas idents this year are focusing on CBBC show KikoRiki, Good considering the characters in that show are anthropomorphic animals, making them awesome for this time of year, I'd assume they will launch tonight in the junction between Doctor Who and Unbreakable, Doctor Who is their highest rating show, so it makes sense to lead into Unbreakable by launching the Christmas idents, Or they could hold it off till tommorow and launch them at Strictly. Whatever works.

Currently still running with the normal dancers idents (when will they be retired, they've been used since 2002)

Emma said on 01 December 2023

Hopefully S4C does something with their new blob mascot, Last year they had TV sets stacked up on top of each other, Then for 10 months they had flickering TV sets, They have finally rebranded and have introduced their first ever mascot: a shape-shifting blob, I hope BBC One rebrand soon, those dancers sure are getting tired, As for the KikoRiki themed Christmas idents, I was told they are launching tonight after Doctor Who, They are being introduced just before Unbreakable.

HouseofFun said on 01 December 2023

Doctor Who ending now, announcement playing "A team of 8 people are pushed to the absolute limit, Unbreakable, our brand new game show is up next, But first, Time to say hello and spend Christmas with the Doctor" I'm not liking this one a whole lot..., They seem to be running a trailer advertising the Doctor Who Christmas special, which revolves around the Doctor having to protect his companions from a man who seeks to find a fob watch (the one The Doctor made himself human with one time), Although just before Doctor Who tonight they had a announcement saying "Christmas is switched on tonight in 1 hour in time for Unbreakable, But first, Is this the end for the Doctor?". Doesn't seem like they are advertising the Christmas pres at all, 

Edit: Christmas pres featuring KikoRiki switched on, First ident shown features Rosa the Pig doing a dramatic ice skate dance (She even uses eyedrops for fake tears) While this is happening we hear dramatic music, The announcer says "Welcome to Christmas on BBC One, Let us entertain you, But now a team of 8 people are being pushed to their absolute limits, Are they Unbreakable?" Not sure why they didn't tell us it was happening untill it did, But still a gorgeous ident, I noticed when the Announcer started talking it sounded as if he was driven to tears by the beauty of Rosa's gorgeous dance. The best thing to come out of this package since the "Lights" christmas idents in 2006, 

Eyes said on 01 December 2023

A beautiful ident, Best thing to come out of the "Rythm and Movement" package since those Planet Earth 3 special idents a few months ago, i feel sad that KikoRiki has not been shown much on CBBC on BBC Two, but I'm glad it's representing the Xmas idents on BBC One (Although I also wish the idents also featured a merman).

Maile said on 01 December 2023

Best Christmas ident in a while, I just love that they have spent it with Rosa and her friends, I hear there is also one featuring Krash the Rabbit opening his presents, I like how they used the usual ident theme (dancing) in that first ident, KikoRiki looks like it's gonna be good this year, but I hope they do more with the style (and maybe finally retire Rythm and Movement idents)

Faramad said on 01 December 2023

News safe ident just on, Basically just a Christmas tree on a snowy background, with a music box Christmas carol playing, on top of that the only thing we see is a sleeping Rosa (We vaguely hear her snoring) Glad they are now using characters in their news safe idents, Announcer said "Now on BBC One, time for the BBC News at 8". Remember the Lights idents the used just before overhauling their promo style in late 2006/early 2007? That was pretty cool, but this is way better. I seem to remember those idents started on a dark street but Christmas lights started turning on before it got to a city center and then people were dancing, It was used again in 2007, And now it seems like the "Project Chameleon" endboard template introduced in July is out the window, at least for now, replaced with a snowy background with a Christmas tree on it. Signs that Rythm and Movement is finally working towards the end.

KikoRiki said on 01 December 2023

We're glad you love our cartoon and the special BBC One idents featuring our characters, 

However, Rosa will be the star, (We will have idents revolving around Chiko, Krash and the others), As to what you said, BBC One are looking into introducing the Circle idents already in use in other countries, so don't expect to see Ballet, Capoeria or Festival/Rave back in the new year. Get used to Kites, Capes, Hippos and Bikes as that's what might be coming after our Christmas campaign, We don't know for certain, but the Dancers might not be coming back after Christmas

WhovianForever: Did everyone catch the Doctor Who Christmas special announcement during the news? I’m so hyped! Can’t wait to see how the Doctor deals with the fob watch saga this holiday season!

LemonyFan: And how about they’re showing the 2004 ‘A Series of Unfortunate Events’ movie!! That’s one of my holiday favorites; the nostalgia is real. 😍🍿

ChristmasCarol: Truly is shaping up to be a wonderful holiday schedule on BBC One. Between Doctor Who and the classic movies, I’m going to be glued to my screen with all the mulled wine and mince pies!

KikoFanatic: Plus, the KikoRiki Christmas idents have been adorable so far! Rosa’s ice skate dance nearly brought tears to my eyes just like the announcer. 🐷⛸ Christmas is definitely switched on!

RetroTV: I’m just here wondering if the new ‘Circle’ idents KikoRiki hinted at is a sign we’re finally saying goodbye to the “Rythm and Movement” package for good. After 20+ years, it’s about time for a major refresh!

NostalgiaNerd: @RetroTV I’m with you on that. The Lights idents back in '06/'07 were beautiful. It’s cool to see they’re still willing to shake things up visually with the news safe ident this year. Sleepy Rosa might just be my new favorite. 😴🎄

SeriesBinger: Doctor Who special + A Series of Unfortunate Events + KikoRiki idents = Best Christmas TV lineup or what? Also, hope BBC One throws in some classic Christmas episodes of our favorite shows. Maybe some ‘Vicar of Dibley’ or ‘Only Fools and Horses’?

CircleCurious: Anyone heard more about these new Circle idents? I love change, and KikoRiki characters have been great, but I’m already excited for what’s coming next. Bring on the new era!

KikoLover: Just saw a new Krash and Chiko ident! They’re having a blast playing in the snow! ❄️🐇 So cute watching Krash throw snowballs while Chiko dodges around. Really captures the winter fun vibes.

SciFiSavvy: Speaking of new idents, did anyone else notice the Dokko the Moose building a laser device ident? It aired right before ‘Doctor Who’ and set the PERFECT tone for sci-fi. Dokko’s always been the tech genius of the group. 🦌🔧

HedgehogAdmirer: @KikoLover Chiko is always adorable, but seeing him in the snow was next level! BBC One knows how to do Christmas idents right. Plus, the animation on these characters in the idents is super impressive this year.

WhovianGuru: @SciFiSavvy Loved the Dokko ident! It’s like a mini episode of ‘Doctor Who’ in itself. Brilliant tie-in to the show’s theme! Makes waiting for the Christmas special even more exciting.

BakeMaster: But guys, let’s talk about the Olga the Owl baking ident. The detail on that gingerbread house she was making! I can almost smell the cookies through the screen. 🦉🍪 It’s the cozy ident I never knew I needed in my life.

HolidayWatcher: Right?! The Olga ident feels like being wrapped in a warm blanket with a hot chocolate in hand. It’s chef’s kiss perfection. BBC One’s going all out with these KikoRiki characters for Christmas.

DoctorFan: Dokko’s ident was like a mini teaser for what’s coming with ‘Doctor Who’. Immersive and full of that tech mystery vibe. I appreciate that BBC One creates these unique intros for thematic continuity.

AnimationAppreciator: Major kudos to the animation team for the KikoRiki idents. As someone who dabbles in animation, the quality is sensational. It’s like a little holiday gift before each show starts!

ChristmasCheer: Can’t get enough of the snow play with Krash and Chiko. It’s absolutely delightful and spreads so much joy. These little moments provided by the idents add a special touch to the holiday season.

SciCompanion: Dokko and his inventions always remind me that anything’s possible with a little imagination. Such an inspiring ident to lead into ‘Doctor Who’. Well played, BBC One! Can’t wait to see what else is in store, I'm gonna miss these idents when new year comes around! 😢

WelcomeToSciFi: The Dokko centric ident really sets the scene for what's to come on ‘Doctor Who’ this Christmas. One of the best things about the KikoRiki set, Far better than the usual dancers, Come to think of it, they have been using the dancers since 2002, so now is a good time for a revamp, I guess 😉

Olgo5: Just saw the Olga ident and damn, Olga really is a talented chef, They always seem to show it before cooking shows, Which ident normally leads into Eastenders, It seems to vary between Krash and Chiko in the snow, Olga cooking and Rosa ice skating. Not sure how this is consistent, But that Rosa one brings  tears to my eyes with her graceful skating, Not sure why the Dokko one only plays before Doctor Who, Maybe because it fits it well

Prolouge:

User1: So, what’s everyone thinking BBC One’s going to pull out of the hat for this year’s Christmas idents? Last year was decent, but I’m hoping for something with a bit more pizzazz.

WhovianForever: I’m crossing my fingers for something Doctor Who-themed! With David Tennant back in the show earlier this year, it would be perfect timing to capitalize on that for the festive season. Can you imagine an ident with the TARDIS all decked out in Christmas lights?

HolidayJoy: That would be cool for us Whovians, for sure, but BBC One usually goes for something that appeals to everyone. Doctor Who is big, but not universally loved by every single viewer. Plus, they tend to lean towards more generic festive symbols.

SeriesBinger: True, they’ll probably aim for something more inclusive. Like a cozy winter scene or playful animation. Maybe bring back some classic characters or even partner with a popular children’s show this time around for wider appeal?

RetroTV: I’ve got a hunch they’ll go with animation this year. It has that universal charm, right? Plus, it would work perfectly with both the kids’ content and the family-friendly vibe they love during Christmas.

User1: That’s an interesting angle. They could get quite creative with animation. Plus, they’re probably due for a change since the “Rythm and Movement” dancers have been around since 2002.

KikoFanatic: What if they teamed up with a show like KikoRiki? The characters are fun, visually engaging, and it’s something that would stand out. Plus, they could do loads with those quirky little animals.

LemonyFan: KikoRiki would be such a cute choice! It could be a hit with children and adults alike if done right. And it’s refreshingly different from usual idents, without relying on something niche like a specific Doctor Who theme.

WhovianForever: Okay, I see your point. Much as I’d love Doctor Who idents, something like KikoRiki could offer that ‘Christmas with the family’ vibe they probably want. I just hope they add a little extra magic this year, you know?

CircleCurious: Whatever they do, I hope it’s something new. We’ve had the “Rythm and Movement” package idents for such a long time. It’s high time for something fresh and exciting to take us into the new year! Write a follow up on January 3 when the Xmas look is still there, but the surrounding presentation has returned to normal, but the promo endboards now have a new BBC One logo in the middle, this poses a major red flag for the Dancers

January 3, 2024

The festive season has drawn to a close, but you wouldn't quite know it by looking at BBC One's lingering Christmas idents. Despite the calendar turning over to January, the lovable characters from KikoRiki are still gracing our screens with their wintery charm and holiday cheer. This has sparked inquiries among viewers, keen to understand the rationale behind the BBC's unusual decision to retain the season-specific imaging past its typical expiration date.

Avid watchers of the channel have noticed, however, that the surrounding presentation has notably shifted back to the standard setup of the earlier months. Promos and trailers have discarded the festive themes and settled back into the usual rhythms, but with a notable change — the endboards now sport a new BBC One logo, positioned quite prominently in the center.

This small but significant adjustment has set the rumor mill into overdrive, leading many to speculate that this is, in fact, the death knell for the iconic "Rythm and Movement" dancers that have been a mainstay for over two decades. The presence of the new logo, without the expected return to the familiar idents of yesteryear, hints at a forthcoming overhaul of the BBC One branding.

On social media, the discussion is in full swing. Nostalgia for the familiar idents is coupled with anticipation for what might be next.

RetroTV: Noticed the new BBC One logo on promo endboards. Could this be a teaser for the new idents? The KikoRiki Christmas animations are still here, which feels odd but may signal a transitional period. Excited and anxious!

AnimationAppreciator: Still seeing Krash and Chiko frolicking in the snow on Jan 3rd. 😅 Maybe BBC One just can’t let go of the holiday vibes. But that new logo? Something's definitely brewing...

NewEraFan: With the updated logo on screen, it's pretty clear BBC One has new branding in the pipeline. Wonder when they'll do the full reveal and retire the dancers? The suspense is killing me!

CircleCurious: Are the KikoRiki idents sticking around because the new Circle package isn't ready yet? Or is BBC One just soft-launching their rebranding? I need answers!

WhovianForever: Honestly, I wouldn't mind if the Doctor Who special had hit the mark so well that they decided to keep a festive feel for a while longer. But that logo is screaming 'change is coming!'

KikoLover: Part of me hoped they'd keep the KikoRiki idents until next Christmas! 😂 But real talk, it's a clear sign we're heading towards a BBC One rebrand. Bittersweet to say goodbye to the dancers, though.

With such a departure from their usual practices, all eyes will be on BBC One during the coming weeks as viewers eagerly await the official unveiling of what's expected to be a fresh, innovative era for Britain's most-watched channel. Will the whispers of the new "Circle" idents prove true, or does the broadcaster have another ace up its sleeve? Only time will tell, but one thing is for certain: change is in the air at BBC One.

Write a tweet where one region did a farewell montage for the dancers on NYE, ending with the Ballet ident, that normally only plays during obit periods, This later led into the Rosa ident which introduced Only an Excuse

🎉📺 As the clock struck midnight, @BBCOneScotland gifted us a heartfelt farewell montage to the "Rythm and Movement" dancers. Ending with the poignant Ballet ident typically reserved for obit periods, it was a classy send-off to an era. An emotional segue into the Rosa ident from #KikoRiki, leading us into the humorous realms of #OnlyAnExcuse. A true blend of reverence and joy as we welcome the new year! #BBCOneFarewellDancers 🕺💃🏽✨
Write a final announcement during the Ballet ident, which begins with "And there you go, a selection of the symbols which have defined BBC One for the past 21 years"

"And there you go, a selection of the symbols which have defined BBC One for the past 21 years. From the energetic to the elegant, these familiar scenes have danced across our screens, accompanying moments of laughter, tears, and everything in between. As we embrace change and look forward to new beginnings, we bid a fond farewell to our 'Rhythm and Movement' dancers. But the show must go on. Join us now, as we continue the celebration with the cherished characters of KikoRiki, bringing their own flavor of joy this New Year's Eve. Next up, the laughter keeps on coming with 'Only an Excuse'."
Write a forum convo where during the announcement the ident segued,

ScreenSavvy: Whoa, did anyone else catch that seamless segue during the announcement on BBC One?

IdentFanatic: Yes! That was beautifully done. They went from the Ballet ident right into the Rosa ident as the announcer was speaking.

ClassicTVLover: It gave me goosebumps! "A selection of the symbols which have defined BBC One for the past 21 years." And then just as the mention of bidding farewell came, it transitioned. Pure class.

VisualArtsGeek: Seriously impressive how they managed that blend. It was like watching a visual representation of passing the torch to the new era.

NewAgeViewer: Honestly, I'm gonna miss those idents. But seeing Rosa skating so gracefully as the Ballet scene faded out was like a symbol for change. Out with the old, in with the new.

IdentFanatic: Totally. That particular moment felt so poignant, right? A nod to the past but firmly stepping into the future. BBC One's presentation team deserves kudos for that edit.

ScreenSavvy: And did you notice how the music just worked with both idents? Like it was orchestrated to be a partner to both the goodbye and the hello. That's attention to detail.

NewAgeViewer: I didn't expect to get emotional over idents, but here we are. 😅

ClassicTVLover: Yep, significant moments like these are what make television history. These are the rebrands we're going to be talking about for years to come.

VisualArtsGeek: Can't wait to see what's next for BBC One's presentation. If they can segue idents that well, the new package is promising.

IdentFanatic: Agreed! Much as we cherish the memories of the dancers, I'm excited for the future. I have a feeling the new idents will be just as iconic. 

NewAgeViewer: Bring it on, I say! BBC One has always been top-notch with their branding, and if this segue is anything to go by, we're in for a treat.
Write a tweet where this is the only time the Ballet ident music has been used on a different ident, and say the ident it was used on

🎶🩰 A historic moment on @BBCOne as the solemn Ballet ident music, traditionally reserved for periods of obituary, graced our screens to a different dance. For the first time, it accompanied the Rosa ident from KikoRiki during a beautifully executed segue, marking a poignant end of an era and an elegant entrance to the new. #BBCOne #FarewellDancers #KikoRiki 🌟
Write a forum convo where the Circles are coming on Jan 18th,

TVFan123: Just read that the Circles are officially coming to BBC One on Jan 18th! The end of an era is truly upon us. Thoughts?

IdentEnthusiast: The Circles, huh? It's going to be a massive change. After more than two decades, it will be strange to switch on the telly and not see the "Rythm and Movement" idents.

BroadcastJunkie: I saw the announcement too. I'm feeling a mix of excitement and nostalgia. Part of me will always miss those dancers, but it's time to freshen things up.

CircleCurious: I'm intrigued to see how the new Circles idents will look. Each rebrand brings its own vibe. They've been iconic elsewhere so I have high hopes for BBC One.

LogoLover: Does anyone know if there's going to be a teaser or some sort of sneak peek? I can't wait to see what the graphics team has cooked up.

VisualVulture: I bet the transition on the 18th is going to be something special. I mean, just look at how they handled the New Year's Eve segue. Set your DVRs, this will be one for the history books!

TechTelly: The preview screenshots I've seen of the Circles look slick. Modern, but with that classic BBC One feel of unity. Curious about the music they'll pair with it.

IdentEnthusiast: Yeah, the music will be key. It's part of what made the "Rythm and Movement" so memorable. They've set the bar high for themselves!

ScreenScribe: Sad to see the old idents go, but you have to adapt and move with the times. If the Circles can capture the spirit of BBC One, then bring it on.

HDViewer: Wondering if the quality will hold up on all the different platforms now. I’ll be checking it out on HD and the iPlayer for sure.

PixelPro: Trust BBC One to not disappoint. I'm expecting smooth animations, vibrant colors, and an eye for detail. They know how much idents can mean to viewers.

TVFan123: True that! Guess we'll all be tuning in with bated breath come the 18th. Here's to hoping the Circles will usher in a new golden age for BBC One presentation! 📺🌟
Write a tweet on Jan 10th saying the idents currently in use, and also say when the majority of the dancers idents were last seen, with a select few having aired as part of a montage on New Years Eve

🔁 On airwaves, @BBCOne cherishes the whimsical KikoRiki idents as we await Circle's debut! The legendary "Rhythm and Movement" dancers, largely absent since December 10th, briefly waltzed back for a nostalgic NYE montage. 🎉🩰 Tune in Jan 18th for the reveal of a new visual symphony—a circle of change! #BBCOne #TVidents #CircleCountdown

Write a tweet on January 18 where Kites is the first shown (kites flying forming a circle), The announcement at 9am says "Good morning and a warm welcome to a new look for BBC One, Kicking things off here's the Andrew Marr show" Also say the ident used at 6am (the final time the Xmas set was used)

🌀🪁 At 9am the sky changed on @BBCOne as the new Kites ident soared, lifting the curtain on a refreshed chapter! The announcement elegantly noted, "Good morning and a warm welcome to a new look for BBC One, kicking things off here's the Andrew Marr show." Farewell to the festive KikoRiki — their last wintery dance graced our screens at 6am. #NewBeginnings #BBCOneRebrand #TheAndrewMarrShow
Write a tweet where the announcer during the day sounds a little bit sad, almost as if he's looking for or missing something

🎙️🔄 Today's @BBCOne announcer carried a hint of nostalgia in their voice — a gentle touch of melancholy amidst the excitement of the new idents. It's as if the familiar dance of the past idents lingers in memory, a cherished rhythm not easily forgotten. #BBCOne #NewIdents #NostalgicNotes
Write a forum convo where CBBC have rebranded 2 times over (going for an abstract look in 2017, then green blobs in 2019)

RetroKid90: So, CBBC's rebranded twice now since the classic era. First, we got the abstract look in 2017, and now these green blobs in 2019. Thoughts?

ToonFanatic: I kind of miss the old style, but the abstract look in 2017 wasn't bad. It had a certain cool edge to it, y'know?

BlobAdmirer: The green blobs have completely grown on me. They're fun, they're vibrant, and they totally scream CBBC!

AnimationAce: Both rebrands had their merits, but they seem quick to move on. I guess they're trying to keep up with how rapidly kids' tastes change?

PixelPundit: Personally, I felt the abstract look captured the creativity of the channel better. The blobs are cute, but maybe a bit too simplistic?

CGI_Guru: Remember, branding's gotta adapt to multiple platforms now, especially with more content going digital. The blobs work well across apps and websites.

NostalgiaKnight: I’ll always have a soft spot for the classic CBBC idents, but I get why they evolve. Keeping things fresh for the next generation of viewers.

GreenGoop: It’s all about energy and playfulness with the green blobs. I think it really hits the mark for today’s young audience.

TweenTVTalk: My little sis loves the blobs. They're basically characters in their own right at this point. It's clever branding that sticks with young minds.

RetroKid90: Fair points, everyone. I do appreciate the need for change. I'm just hopeful they'll stick with the blobs a bit longer. These rapid transitions can be jarring.

ChannelChanger: True, consistency can be good, especially for a kids’ channel. Helps build that sense of identity and loyalty over time.

DesignDude: It's all about striking a balance between staying relevant and maintaining a recognizable brand. Let's see how long these blobs stick around.

TweenTVTalk: Exactly! As long as the shows are still good and the branding isn't off-putting, CBBC is doing its job. Kids these days will probably remember the green blobs with nostalgia in the future!

PixelPundit: Actually, I think the blobs might stay. They've got merch potential, and they're memorable. That's half the battle won in branding.
